note: No human-messages.json sessions exist for this date range (sessions start Mar 20). Era 2 was driven by Cursor IDE agent and Claude Code. This chunk is populated from commit metadata and deep-era archaeological analysis.
author: Pastorsimon1798 + Simon
narrative_arc: A single day transforms everything — Cursor IDE writes code at machine speed, the project finds its identity as 'Liminal', and a major intelligence layer is merged
key_events: 4 new creative coding generators (noise/flow fields, GLSL, Three.js, LLM music)
deep_analysis_summary: Era 2 is a single day that compressed more change into Liminal than any other 24-hour period. The codebase went from 416 files to 2,180 files -- a 5.2x expansion. A Cursor IDE agent wrote 15 commits in six minutes (37-second average interval). The Hydra intelligence layer brought 10,146 lines of multi-model collaborative AI. The project was renamed from Atelier to Liminal. The day reveals three distinct modes: machine-speed generation (00:29-00:35), human-speed curation (10:26-14:35), and strategic acts (21:30-22:40). The three-interface progression -- OpenClaw phone sketching, Cursor IDE cleanup, Claude Code deep work -- was itself a discovery: the developer was learning which AI interface fit which kind of work in real time.
decisions_made: Turn Cursor IDE agent loose on entire codebase autonomously
Commit per logical unit despite machine speed
Temporarily remove CI workflows for velocity (never confirmed restored)
Port Hydra from Python to TypeScript (328 new tests claimed)
Rename Atelier to Liminal (identity declaration, not cosmetic)
decisions_rejected: Sequential human review of agent output (audited after, not during)
Kai-style methodical scaffolding (Cursor worked in broader strokes)
identity_shift: The rename from Atelier (artist's workshop) to Liminal (threshold, space between states) tracked a philosophical shift: from making things in a workshop to existing in the space between intention and creation.
critical_finding: The developer tried three AI interfaces across two days and converged on Claude Code. Liminal was not just the thing being built. It was the test bed for figuring out how to build with AI.
